THE COURT IS NO LONGER IN RECEIPT OF THE COURTESY COPIES. EACH PARTY IS ORDERED TO DELIVER A NEW COURTESY COPY OF ALL PAPERS THEY FILED TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JANUARY 5, 2015. ANY PARTY WHO CONTESTS A TENTATIVE RULING MUST SEND AN EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WITH A COPY TO ALL OTHER PARTIES BY 4PM STATING, WITHOUT ARGUMENT, THE PORTION(S) OF THE TENTATIVE RULING THAT THE PARTY CONTESTS. THE SUBJECT LINE OF THE EMAIL SHALL INCLUDE THE LINE NUMBER, CASE NAME AND CASE NUMBER. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order On Further Discovery And Sanctions For Failure To Obey Court Order [Discovery]
Off Calendar May-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MELANIE MASSEY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF MICHAEL H. KIM, TEL. NO. (650)697-8899 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHARLES LOUIS THOMPSON APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOSE MURILLO'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order On Further Discovery And Sanctions For Failure To Obey Court Order [Discovery] - The Motion is DENIED without prejudice to renewal. The Motion as presented is procedurally defective, in that less than the required notice was given for a regularly-scheduled motion served by mail (as indicated by the moving party's Proof of Service). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge Pro-tem: David Helbraun; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/jpt

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One);
Off Calendar Jan-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael H. Kim, Esq. of the Law Office of Michael H. Kim, Tel. 650.697.8899, for plaintiff and moving party;
Charles L. Thomas, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart, PC, Tel. 415.536.3430, for defendant and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: Both parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOSE MURILLO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) is granted as to the identification of all non-exempt food services and restaurant employees employed by Joe's Union L.P. during the alleged class period; it is denied as to all other information sought by interrogatories 4 - 5. This denial is without prejudice. Defendant's response will be filed within thirty (30) days of the date of this order. The Court will impose no monetary sanctions. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Jaimie Hopp, CSR #13751, Tel. 925.759.0770.

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Sets One And Two);
Off Calendar Jan-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael H. Kim, Esq. of the Law Office of Michael H. Kim, Tel. 650.697.8899, for plaintiff and moving party;
Charles L. Thomas, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart, PC, Tel. 415.536.3430, for defendant and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: Both parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling, modified as follows: PLAINTIFF JOSE MURILLO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Sets One And Two) is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant's objections to requests nos. 25,26,27,37,38,39,40,41&48 are overruled and Defendant will provide further responses within thirty days (30) days from the date of this order. The motion is denied as to requests for production nos. 42 -47, and nos. 49-52. No monetary sanctions will be assessed. This denial is without prejudice to plaintiff making further request for production of documents upon a showing of good cause. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Jaimie Hopp, CSR #13751, Tel. 925.759.0770.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative , Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICAHEL H. KIM, TEL. NO. (213)738-6005 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LISA LIN GARCIA & ATTY. MICHELLE FALCONER OF THE LITTLER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 677-4089 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ORIGINAL JOE'S INC. AND JOE'S UNION L.P'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative , Summary Adjudication - The court grants summary judgment in favor of Original Joe's, Inc. The declaration of Marie Duggan demonstrates that Original Joe's, Inc. does not have any role in the operation of the restaurant and it is not plaintiff's employer. Plaintiff fails to present any controverting evidence regarding that issue. The court denies plaintiff's request for a continuance per CCP 437c(h). The outstanding discovery is focused on finding a new class representative. The discovery is unrelated to the employer/employee issue. The court denies without prejudice the motion as to Joe's Union, L.P. The settlement does not comply with CRC 3.769 and 3.770 because the court never approved the settlement. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098, TEL. NO. (415)717-9768
